Supreme Court verdict clarifies scope of environmental clearances

By Legal Correspondent | Updated: July 5, 2026 09:15 IST

The Supreme Court on Thursday delivered a significant verdict clarifying the scope of environmental clearances required for infrastructure projects located near ecologically sensitive zones.

The bench held that project proponents must obtain clearance before starting any preparatory construction activity, rejecting the argument that preliminary site work falls outside the clearance requirement.

Legal experts said the ruling would have significant implications for ongoing infrastructure projects across several states, particularly those situated near forest and coastal zones.

The judgment also directed the environment ministry to review its notification framework and submit a compliance report within six months, the court's order stated.
